Full_Review: All good apart from the kettle in the room had tea leaves in it and hadn t been cleaned The Dawaat restaurant in the hotel served very tiny portions   The breakfast was a good selection Concierge staff very helpful and knowledgable about London Excellent location with staff who were always polite and willing to help
Nothing   Only stayed one night Everything was purfect as usual We were upgraded to a standard double The breakfast was excellent Friendly helpful staff
Very small rooms without air con are VERY hot Lots of road noise if you open the windows to try and cool down Disappointing aspect   Good location good breakfast

We cannot give full marks for everything as we are never in the hotel for long enough We have never chosen to eat breakfast or an evening meal in the hotel Choosing an upgrade for a room has worked for us a bit extra space I would prefer a feather pillow but for one night everything is fine   We choose The Strand Palace because of its location The staff are friendly and informative We only ever stay overnight

It might have been better for my nerves not to have had the fire alarm sounding at 9 00 in the evening for a false alarm but better safe than sorry   The attitude of the staff nothing was ever too much trouble Spotlessly clean and comfortable

Very poor customer service at reception I reached there at midnight wanting some rest Staff took some half an hour to go through checks and booking in Such a poor service honestly I dislike from my heart   Location was good

I didn t appreciate the fire alarm going off at 9pm This is the second time this has happened at this hotel Someone smoking in the bedroom With computer systems these days they should be able to tell which room is the problem instead of evacuating the whole hotel Never had this happen before anywhere else   Location location it is perfect for the galleries and theatres I want to visit

Lack of air conditioning In a 4 star hotel it should be a given If premier Inn can have it why can t you In this day and age it s a must   Great location for the theatres and London city Facilities were great and so was the food and cocktails

Service at breakfast was slightly slow no fridge in the bedroom had to discuss advertised discount on food and drinks at check out although it was granted in the end  Comfortable large bedroom warm welcome good bars

I cannot work out what the doormen are for They can be replaced by a small wooden wedge or an auto door One of them spends his time yelling at the staff   Everything We always stay here when in London Raymond the concierge is the greatest
It was a hot weekend so air conditioning would have been good although there was a fan in the room which was a bonus and helped   The location could t have been any better

The bed is quite small for two people   Very very good location so convenient to the monuments But the area has many homeless around quite smelty The bed is quite small for two people The breakfast is so great typical English breakfast and many choices included
There was nothing l didn t like   The rooms were very comfortable The reception staff were very efficient The hotel was in a very convenient location
Staff were clearly over stretched and a little rushed and impatient   I liked the bath robes and slippers the little extras and the excellent level of cleanliness

Nothing Our most recent stay was excellent   This hotel is conveniently located in central London It is easy to walk to all the major central London tourist hot spots Underground stations are within a short walk and bus stops are adjacent to the hotel Staff are friendly polite and helpful The hotel is always spotlessly clean Rooms are well appointed and have everything you need for a stay in London

Single rooms tend to be small and with no view I understand the reason for this lack of space in central London Very high price   Efficient and very pleasant reception staff clean modern room comfortable bed good and varied breakfast Always nice to stay in this hotel I like the atmosphere and it is a great location for sightseeing shopping and theatres in London

No air conditioning on what was a very hot August weekend quite stuffy as a result The lifts took ages to come only one ever seemed to be in use at a time despite there being three of them   Fantastic location a few minutes walk from many top London points of interest Very friendly and professional service Free computer printer for tickets boarding pass many hotels charge for this so this was a good plus point

The room It was more like a student room on a university campus than a hotel room No curtains but wooden shutters not solid period ones bed pushed against the wall as this was the only way a double would fit in no air conditioning generally other hotels locally represent better value for money and much better comfort   Location only

Wasn t impressed with the breakfast over priced and bacon hard and chewy croissants hard and taste horrible this is the only thing that let the hotel dwn everything else was great  Check in fast and friendly all staff friendly when in and around hotel room lovely comfy clean and quite

The perfectness of London is also the downside no need for hotels to even have the ambition to stand out and make an effort room too hot too old bed too small I had a double with my daughter bed was 180cm I am 186cm and 120cm wide and the view of room 892 was just redicoulous way overpriced   London is fantastic Strand next to Covent Garden is the perfect location for my daughter who visited the city for the first time and me as a London resident for many years
No air conditioning in standard rooms be warned if visiting in hot weather Room a little on the small side  Great service and location Good mix of facilities available
Room size with ref to bed position which was up against the wall at one side Should have booked a larger room I think Will do so next time   All likes already stated
The bathroom was poorly equipped and too small The shower flooded the room in spite of correctly using the curtain in the bath Pillows very uncomfortable   Location

I would have loved a small fridge in the room   The staff were welcoming and friendly I needed help with printing out an email that I should have brought from home Reception were happy to print it for me which was a great relief

My husband and I thoroughly enjoyed our stay at the Strand Palace It was our first stay there Everything about the hotel was lovely The choice of breakfast was superb displayed and we l organised We enjoyed the comfort of the lounge for coffee and a cake away from the hustle and bustle of Covent Garden The location was ideal a short walk to Joe Allens for evening meal and a drink in the cocktail bar next door first We are looking forward to our next stay

I got very bad reception people who was not friendly to treat me when I asked a coulp of hours check out They can changed price dramatically and the attitude was very bad Sorry have such kind of memories in this hotel since I am the one ofenly travring around word   The people who worked Conciege and house Maid

The room could have been a bit larger but this is Central London   so convenient for the theatre right in the centre of things Room a little small but had all the facilities we needed Breakfast was good

Bed pillows were very hard the sink was cracked water did not empty from sink very well TV control was not working right some staff did not understand English very well Got different answers from different staff concierge did not have enough information to help you I booked it as a 4 but would only class it as a 3 was quite disappointed Would not recommend it as a 4 to friends   Breakfast was nice good area

whilst the cleaning staff were v friendly the rooms could do with a good scrub especially the bathroom and taps etc with the Strand Palace you are definitely paying for location as the hotel is expensive for what you get I found one of the concierge and one doorman quite rude to guests
The room It was described as a cosy room A better description would be a sauna Very hot even with the window open and a fan on   Staff very friendly and helpful Location excellent

Could find nothing I didnt like was truly impressed and pleased with the whole weekend experience  Tmy toom was a single and having feared it would be like a box i was pleasantly surprised the room was spacious as was the adjoining bathroom My friends shared a cosy room and likewise pleasantly surprised and pleased at the space I cannot fault this hoyel and the location was the best for all tourist lications we walked everywhere
Rooms furniture is quite old and no air conditioning in the rooms and quite loud noise combing through the windows even though they are closed  Friendly staff and great location

paying for ice come on what is that all about just add 1 to every room and make it free Had to troop down to the concierge get him to make change troop back up to 6th floor etc   Good location comfortable hotel tried to help when we arrived to check in early but room not ready got changed in the gym

The hotel was very noisy in the morning not the fault of the hotel probably more other guests   The location is right in the heart of London My room was ready when I arrived at 10 30 am even though it said check in was from 2 pm and I had been upgraded from a cosy single to a standard single at no extra cost The hotel was far more glamorous than the website suggests

Slow check in Extremely small room though it was an economy single so I didn t expect it to be large Windows hard to open   Great location handy for theatres Covent Garden restaurants easy to get to the South Bank Tate Modern and Shakespeare s Globe good breakfast staff generally friendly and helpful Nice new bathroom

Staff are extremely welcoming and professional Really made you think you d want to return in the future Fantastic location Covent Garden theatreland and Soho on the doorstep Excellent public transport links buses outside the dor tube five minutes walk

Queueing for breakfast Staff in breakfast room a little lacking manners when you are carrying your food to table Although food good bacon very overcooked   Convenient location helpful and attentive staff and suitable overnight hotel for central London visit Lovely hotel

The hotel has been refurbished and I feel a bit sorry that no air conditioning has been installed in the rooms especially because the windows can only be opened a little   I liked everything and breakfast is simply super As I am a theaterbuff the location of the hotel is super Buses and tube are within easy reach and many City highlights are within walking distance Staff are very friendly I stayed at the hotel at least 7 times and I hope to be back soon
The bed was rather too firm for me   The room space had been well thought out for a budget room The breakfasts were excellent Apart from an occasional plumbing noise the soundproofing was good

The staff were extremely polite friendly and helpful The room was spotless and the huge bed was very comfortable There was a great variety of food for breakfast and it was all well cooked and attractively presented We can also recommend The Carvery both for quality of food and value for money We particularly appreciated the way we could leave our luggage with the concierge after we had checked out so that we didn t have to lug it around London for the rest of the day

I had unfortunately used Booking com and made a slight error When I called the hotel one member of staff said I would definitely need to contact Booking com again I tried but could not get a response from them so I called the hotel again and another member of staff told me she could make the amendment after all I was grateful that the second member of staff knew what she was doing Next time I shall book directly with the hotel as they give you incentives when you book through them   My room was changed to a quieter part of the hotel as last time I stayed I was woken up very early by bottles being emptied also this time I did not have an adjoining room which was great as you can hear everything through the walls So I finally got the peace and quiet I deserved

My room was billed as cosy which meant very very small but it met my needs as a single traveler and the value was very good considering that this is a full service hotel on The Strand in walking distance to theaters museums and many shops and restaurants

The parsnips on the carvery were partially burnt My room did not have air conditioning although they did give me a fan When I book through your site I usually get an ordinary room This time I got a single which was rather small although the bathroom was very nice   The hotel location suited my activities I could use the bus across the road to get to most places I wanted The staff were also very pleasant Breakfast was good

Rooms adequate in size but beware of lowest price level as rooms are tiny If that is what your budget allows then price is very reasonable for city centre  Perfect location for all London attractions and priced fairly for such a central location
very happy and surprised when checking in that we had been upgraded and a request made by a family member to the hotel was met they more than fulfilled the ask A very happy newly engaged couple

The place is overpriced for the type of room you get If you are single you are better off in a hostel You will get a better accommodation there The rooms have no air conditioning and are closed off from air making it very hot in the rooms and uncomfortable The beds are saggy and are worse than some hostels Not what I would expect from a so called 3 or 4 star hotel I would avoid this place  Good Location

Room on the Strand road side and having stayed before this was the first time we stayed on the road won t do it again at night you can really hear the traffic and did affect getting to sleep  Location fantastic for getting around
Great location for walking around the centre of London and close to Trafalgar Square Handy for museums theatres and shops I ll use it again next time I m in London
Very noisy cleaners early in the morning and kept trying to get into the room Maybe update cleaners on who has and hasn t checked out   clean comfortable and value for money

The room was tiny no air conditioning so very warm through the night Everything was in the way of the plugs A double bed but against a wall not easy to get in and out off They call it the Cosy Double don t be fooled cosy tiny   The location was good
Room either too hot or too noisy It overlooked the inside area which had some very noisy fans running 24h With closed windows the room was too hot Impossible to get a good night s sleep
not even small refridgerator small windows not allowing the air to circulate not clear information about the services in the hotel booklet long time to wait for a lift  great location
Great staff at breakfast and a wide selection of foods Also the hotel gave my husband and I a Happy Birthday muffin which was in the room for us

Rooms a bit small for the price we paid but well appointed I guess if you had masses of luggage it could be a problem This wasn t an issue for us as we were over nighters   it is a very comfortable hotel with bars and restaurants within striding distance The hotel facilities are superb if you are feeling to lazy to go out Location is great
The WiFi never worked in my room   The location and cleanliness I was upgraded to a double room from the single room I had booked so making it good value for the centre of London

Very uncomfortable and stiflingly hot I contemplated sleeping in the lobby as it seemed to be the only place that had air conditioning Not worth a fraction of the price it commands   Very close to Covent Garden

Room and bathroom too small The cost didn t justify what was probably the smallest bathroom I have ever seen in a hotel Other people I spoke to in the bar said the same so it wasn t just our room   Location

In order to check in two hours early to our cleaned and ready room the front desk required a 20 pound fee Perhaps worth it but we skipped the option and went to have lunch There s no fee to have them hold your luggage   The breakfast buffet offered a convenient tasty option that was a really good buy The fitness room in the hotel was adequate to provide a good workout in a pleasant setting The hotel is a terrific buy in terms of its good amenities and the excellent location
Ridiculously small room even for London no A C bit too expensive for what you get  Location cleanliness housekeeping staff check in and check out Luggage storage after check out

Asked for room to be decorated for birthday Was told it could be possible Asked what time we would be out On our return nothing had been done   Had a gift of breakfast in bed but the pastries were very dry Bed not big enough for a 6 foot plus male

Not being able to open windows very hot in bedroom bathroom  We have stayed here before great location and staff mostly helpful Lady on check in was really helpful as we had a few rooms and she helped sort our booking out Thank you again and we look forward to returning
Room not ready when arrived Second morning breakfast delayed by lack of staff We booked an executive room and felt it wasn t value for money   Location is Excellent Cleanliness is excellent

The room was absolutely airless to the point of stifling although I had an extre night booked I decided to leave one night early as I couldn t stand another night of roasting in hell I had a single room one night facing a wall so changed to a double not much bigger still facing a wall and like an oven Also they were very unbending about changing one night s accommodation for me to another although in money terms it wouldn t have changed anything for them and the room was available it would have made another loyal customer for them it is not great to shoot themselves in the foot by some silly rules that can be adjusted in special circumstances Every customer counts and word of mouth is still the best or worst advert The hotel is called grand but in fact it is a very tired hotel and needs an update badly I would be spending my money somewhere else in the future  The staff were nice but scatty two mornings my tea and gluten free bread took over 20 minutes to get to me not great when you need to be at work
small room with very noisy extractor fans from outside our room also the fans generated heat which made our room unbearable to sleep in  location was excellent
the size of the room and bed and that it was againt the wall there was no aircon only a fan which was too noisey   the location the efficiency of the staff

First room was noisy but then I was moved to another one which was perfect   Nice food cosy room excelente location really close to Covent Garden and Trafalgar Square Served by Picadilly Line excelent if you re arriving from Heathrow Airport

Breakfast was good but not great Like many London hotels very busy and they struggle at times to deal with the numbers at reception and in the restaurant   Great location Easy walk to Trafalgar Square or Theatres Lots of restaurants around Had an executive room which was a good size quiet and comfortable This is our second stay and equally as good

There was a heat wave in London and the rooms were not air conditioned It was hard to sleep and impossible to rest in the room We had to hang in the lobby to stay cool The rooms were also very small for twin rooms   I liked the service and I liked the facilities the lobby the restaurant the concierge etc The location was perfect for us
Everything was fine echo the view from the window but that could be changed Perhaps air conditioning could be installed but a fan was provided  Room was spacious and well equipped and very quiet
Rooms were far to small described as COZY small should be the word not cozy Bath rooms dated as well So for the money I won t be staying again   location

We went for an executive suite However this was just a normal room by all accounts Fabulously big bed but some furnishings looking a bit tired and the bathroom was tiny Also for an executive suite the room itself was actually quite small   The location is fabulous very close to Covent Garden and the theatres Easy to get to and great customer service by all the staff who served us Lots of facilities available too The breakfast which came with the Executive Room was very good indeed

For cost would have hoped to have air con in the room A fan was provided but not sufficient to keep the room cool without the window open which meant street noise Bed small and uncomfortable   Super location close to tube and local attractions Good restaurants close by Well presented room

Not a major problem but there was a bit of a wait at reception when checking in and out Another member of staff to receive guests would have been welcome   Clean good facilities pleasant helpful staff good food good location
Air conditioning in room We were there during a heat wave   Location was perfect was right in the middle of everything Walking distance to all places of interest
Queuing up for breakfast or being guided to a makeshift breakfast space is no fun  The perfect location Walkable to all the main sites shopping and right across the best musicals in town

Bathroom very compact shower over bath awkward and not that easy to control water direction Toilet in bathroom needed fixing during our stay poor flush Room very hot   The location near theatres Covent garden is excellent Concierge staff were especially pleasant helpful Very good breakfast Cleanliness A Would stay again if visiting London

Do not rely on the pictures as a guide as the rooms are smaller than pictured Not great value for money in terms of the room but we were not in it that often so it didn t affect us too much   Great central location Very clean and upmarket lobby area and bar restauraunt area

This older property only has air conditioning in the lobby so in summer and warmer days of spring you ll be using the provided oscillating fan and an open window to cool off There is a heating radiator for winter I think my son was a little disappointed that the rooms were rather Spartan compared to the sparkling lobby but a hotel should never be the reason for your travel   Hotel location is terrific and central Covent Garden for seeing London near bridges museums The Eye theatre so much within walking distance and the rest a short cab ride or tube ride away The rooms are clean and well taken care of for an older property Beds are okay
Old fashioned rooms and no air conditioning  Great facilities and breakfast was excellent Female staff member in gin palace was great and dealt with some difficult customers very well

Lower price of the breakfast would have made this perfect or possibly some discount to breakfast places nearby   The gentlemen at the front doors I just love them created a real British feeling and made us so happy and appreciated quests Also the lobby is nice and peaceful a real contrast to one who steps in from the Strand
Room wasn t ready when we turned up to check in and they didn t seem to have a clue why and we waited for an hour to get the room key   Location spot on

It was much too hot in the room which was on the second floor With the window open there was something industrial pumping out noise and heat all night just under the window so sleep was impossible There was no air conditioning and when I inquired having booked a single room I was told that there was only air conditioning in double rooms and I should have booked that This was not made apparent when I booked online and also would have meant a significant cost increase in an already expensive hotel Also they charged for ice the ice machine on the 1st floor cost 1 for a small tub of ice I have never stayed in a hotel which charged for ice from the ice machine   The mattress felt good very firm And the towels were large
With being only overnight we did not have any qualms   Stayed in a variety of hotels in past and had the most uncomfortable of sleep the Strand offered quietness and a very comfortable night s rest

1 rooms were smaller then a prison cell 2 invest in some air conditioning boiling hot 3 had to have a very warm shower as the showers were broken 4 receptionist wouldn t let us check into our rooms unless all our guests we re present we booked 2 rooms as one was a surprise for the birthday boy she was rude until we explained the situation 5 rooms were not ready by the time we got there and this was at 6 pm 6 bed was very small and uncomfortable 7 definitely a waste of 380 00 for 2 rooms wouldn t even pay 50 00 for both the rooms  Lobby was presentable everything else was awful

It s a huge hotel with many rooms on inner courts Because of a late booking we had a room on the first floor with barely any view on the court wall and sunlight next to the maids room quite noisy unless you plan to study Polish 4 star price for a one star situation Check the room floor and situation if you can  Although the chief receptionist was a cold poker face employees were very nice and helpful The gym is a real plus The hotel is well located to visit most of London although you will have to walk a bit for the nearest tube stations Temple and Embankment The building itself is not astonishing and brand new but the room are very well cleaned and bathrooms are convenient and neat It s close to many theaters if you plan to attend shows Covent Garden is a 5 minutes walk from the hotel

A bit noisy with window open but fine with earplugs   Clean and modern Unbeatable location Room was small but perfectly adequate if you re out and about most of the time Good breakfast and pleasant and efficient staff
room was expensive but as we paid for location it was to be expected I suppose   location to convent garden which we love was excellent lots shops pubs and restaurants

Restaurant for breakfast was quite small given the size of the hotel however food was good   Staff were exceptionally friendly we didn t eat in the hotel for dinner but can strongly recommend the gin palace for a nightcap yumi

Found the breakfast area rather crowded and cramped Food just average Charging 5 pounds per night to leave luggage is a joke also having to pay for a map the first hotel ever to do this  Excellent location comfortable beds and clean Friendly and helpful staff

Nothing all good  There was so much to like about this hotel Traditional feel in a modern setting location close to Covent Garden theatres and the Thames Staff were very professional but also very friendly and genuinely polite Very good value for money including drinks a food that was not part of the booking an absolute gem of a hotel
Breakfast was horrible could of done with more healthy options ie Museli don t waste your money Room was too dark and gloomy   Great bathroom and bath Staff were friendly and helpful

The room was too hot couldn t open the windows wide enough to get some fresh air in so neither of us got much sleep The view was bad into some scruffy courtyard with other windows but that didn t bother us that much I guess we got what we paid for   Loved the location the breakfast and the decor of the hotel The staff were very friendly and polite
Second time to stay here 4th floor is far more dated than the 2nd Smaller old fashioned room Could hear the people next door   The receptionist was very welcoming and friendly The location is great

This Hotel is advertised as 4 star The room we booked was a standard room It was small the bed was small pillows uncomfortable no air con and the room was hot if the window was open the noise from the street was very loud problems with the plumbing very noisy

It was amazing the staff were great the facilities were very good it was quiet Not to hot and not to cold very good shower Will definitely go back there on our next visit to London everything was within walking distance and if you didn t want to walk buses and trains were very easy to get to I haven t got any bad comments or regrets the whole visit was brilliant

Small expensive rooms 3 toilet repairs in 6 days Maintenance twice tried and failed to solve TV tuning problems friends had similar TV problem Hotel lounge refused to show Euro 2016 on their TV 1 of 2 treadmills and 1 of 2 bikes were down for maintenance   Beautiful hotel with nice staff in terrific location Small but OK fitness center Manager gave us 15 GBP voucher and bottle of sparkling wine to partly make up for maintenance problems but would not adjust room rate

I asked for a quiet room Away from lifts It was away from lifts but it wasn t quiet There was noise from either air conditioning units or electrical unit It woke me up a couple of times I cant put my finger on what the noise was but it did disturb my sleep Left luggage not very well run bit chaotic Little bit dismissive surly   Location and check in lady great Clean room and bed very comfortable

Some of the items from the breakfast where cold   The location the availability of the staff Some of the staff on the day of my departure from the hotel went beyond the limit and showed excellent customer service
Lighting in bathroom could be better  Warm and friendly greeting speedy check in Room comfortable and spotless dressing gowns and slippers what a treat Super breakfast lots of lovely fresh fruit
the breakfast was okay the vegetables didn t look edible the tomatoes weren t cooked the room was way to hot and noisy  The staff were friendly

No air con meant the room would most likely be hot during high Summer   The room was very clean the location fantastic the bath tub was perfect for soothing tired limbs All in all a very enjoyable experience of London

Noisy pipes in the bathroom But they got a plumber Housekeeping knocked really early on the day I was checking out   The bar staff and carvery staff were lovely The blackout shutters on the window made it easier to sleep and the room was quiet

lunch could have been complementary similar to other hotels   the best thing about the place is its location the facility is ok although my room was a bit small but overall i had a pleasant stay and a good experience

Room was very hot and only a quilt on the bed Would have been nice to have the option of a sheet instead Other than that the hotel was perfect for what we wanted and was great value for money   Location
Older staff disjointed Night staff some trouble with English and basic service Small rooms No AC  Great location close to palace Thames attractions and great pubs
Nothing  Breakfast is excellent plus bar food although bit pricy but it is London and no dearer than anywhere else Cant fault the hotel and cleanliness of rooms best i know

Evening dinner could have been better Told I was upgraded but in reality it was a disability room on the first floor Traffic noise a minor irritation   Breakfast was good Location good for my business purpose
There was no toilet brush in the toilet and only cleaned on 2nd day and bedsheets were not changed for the whole 3 days  Concierge porter service great location
More choice of non cooked food Fresh fruit Healthier options  Room very small for two people not value for money Very poor breakfast not worth the money so glad we only paid 10

We did ask for early check in due to having tickets to a matinee at the theatre so we could get ready and this was agreed in advance and a payment of 20 was agreed for this but on arrival they had no rooms available for early check so this was cancelled   The location was brilliant walking distance to everywhere and plenty to do and see Hotel was lovely and staff were very helpful
Room was quite basic and small even though we was given an upgrade for the price we paid was hoping for something a bit more spacious  Location excellent

The bed was not comfy the room cleaners woke me up The bar does not sell good old British pints of beer Only expensive bottle beer at this hotel How can you have a London Hotel not selling British pints of beer   The location and price

Cleanness of rooms could ve been better Room was tidy but could use some refresh Facilities like the gym was a misunderstanding with not all functional gear and ppl queue to use it   Location the main option I ve chosen this hotel wasn t disappointed Staff was helpful and understanding

The single room had no a c No access to open the minute window The bathroom was so small it was difficult to move around Bed was hard Room was tired could do with updating  Good size TV in the room It was very quiet considering location Friendly staff

The sound insulation was very poor I could hear exactly what was going on in the next bathroom and felt like I was sleeping in the corridor with the noise from passing people Also the solid wooden shutters at the window made the room really dark   The room was very clean and tastefully decorated and the bed was really comfy All staff I came across were polite friendly and helpful
Front desk said local calls were free but then got charged 20p for two calls No big deal but misleading   Very friendly staff Polite Helpful Location really perfect for tourists

The room was a little dated for the price you pay The restaurant manager was a little pushy trying to turn tables when it was not that busy for breakfast I noticed some guests were not to happy with him   The location was perfect for walking and getting around London And the staff at the front desk and concierge were professional and quite personable

Telephone was dead toilet flush is not that good We paid about 300USD night feel little bit overpriced   The location can not be better we could walk many iconic places and access to several lines in 5min walk I am really glad we stayed at this hotel Concierge was excellent

Our room was called a cosy room which was basically small which we didn t mind However Unfortunatley the walls a paper thin we had very noisy people next door we had to complain the last night ask the staff to sort it out which they did But they continued been loud then decided to have the tv on It was after 4 when all went quite we could then get some sleep   The hotel was pretty central to where we wanted to be easy to get to the tube or buses
Mirrors are too far away to put makeup on properly Tv fixed to wall in wrong position Not really that much of a pribl though as we didnt watch it much   Liked location and helpful staff

Hard bed and this has been our 3rd time to have a cosy room The rooms are getting smaller each time and the bed against the wall is NOT cosy for two people Will not be staying again   good breakfast choices
Room overlooking the Strand was quite noisy when the windows were open   Was upgraded which is always nice Doorman was very helpful getting me a taxi in torrential rain

Bit on the expensive side for just one but I m worth it   Totally convenient for my trip to Art Galleries and meeting friends Fresh juicey apple available on desk at reception which was a nice touch after walking down to the Strand from Euston Plus I was given fresh water to drink while I checked in
Not value for money Room like a cell No air conditioning If there hadn t been a fan to use I would have left as humidity affects my health   Staff friendly
Everything was great  We have used this hotel several times the staff are very polite and helpful last time we stayed they upgraded us to executive room which was great food is also very good
Could have done with a fridge in the room but wasn t a major problem   Super location excellent for theatre area Very helpful staff Well worth a stay

Hotel rooms need a make over e g some bathroom fittings were missing and must be at least 20 years old Room was also small   Great location especially for theatres galleries and London sites generally

Rooms are very small but they are ok for London standards Lobby decor is very old fashioned with fish tanks   Best location ever near metro station Covent Garden many many many good restaurants expensive and cheap good service for what the hotel is its location etc it offers a GREAT cost benefit relation I would stay in Strand Palace again and will recommend it to everybody
The breakfast Being woken up at 1 00 and then 2 00 in the morning Had to have the window open no air con being on the second floor hear all the noises of the centre courtyard   Pleasant staff

The breakfasts needs a shake up Fruit not always fresh breakfast too sameish Especially for people who stay more than three nights I know it was part of our package and we chose that I suggest an opt out option should be allowed   HELPFUL STAFF CLEAN HOTEL

The cooked breakfast had been sitting under the heat lamps for too long so had dried out but the rest was great croissants and fruit cheese and ham etc   Comfortable bed cosy rooms are small but not an issue as a base when you re staying in London The 8th floor had a great view of the storms across London too

Choice of room far too small We did not understand how small a cosy room would be Next time we will be more careful Noisy fan somewhere near our room   Breakfast was too cool the first morning I told them Next morning fine Friendly staff throughout Sheer convenience of the location Choice of tubes stations and the contactless card makes journeying on the Underground so easy for northern Scots as well as others I presume Great tailor next door G R Check them out for yourselves Walking is easy around here Covent Garden has lots going on any day and you can always stop to be entertained in the pedestrian area
The queue to get into breakfast   The room was a good size shower was powerful The breakfast fresh plentiful and the scrambled egg so moist and fluffy

Think drinks but pricey and room a bit small toilet squashed behind the door but was ok for 1 night   The hotel is lovely staff very friendly although wasn t sure to tip the porter and think annoyed him But he was helpful and pleasant It s good location for the west end if going to shows and Covent Garden is a short walk away It s quite expensive as in London and thought room was bit small for the price but clean and tidy The man who you give bags too was brilliant and very helpful Couldn t fault the place Great place to go also good was nice for breakfast

Can not understand why when I booked over the internet that I was quoted 20 per head for breakfast which I was willing to pay 80 for four breakfasts on my visa card and when I got to the hotel and said I would like to pay hotel and breakfast in cash instead of my Visa card Breakfast dropped straight away to 10 per head Also I booked an early arrival booking lunch time nothing was mentioned about the extra 20 fee I had to pay until I arrived at the hotel I think you should give better details to those hotel sites internet you have your details on I will stay again at your hotel but will certainly not include breakfast until I arrive and pay cash for them at 10 per head cheaper Mx  Breakfast s was good the room was very clean and nice hotel staff very helpful and pleasant We enjoyed our stay at The Strand Palace thank you Mx
Lack of natural daylight No fridge in room Although bed comfortable I thought the room was overpriced   Bed was extremely comfortable Breakfast was excellent
I was really surprised and impressed by how quiet the room was considering how busy it was outside The food was really good and the staff were really helpful and friendly

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Carpet stained quite poor Lack of facilities in the hotel generally Check in staff tried to apply the wrong room rate to the room both on check in and check out I e no lesson learned first time around English language skills of staff could be a little better Not a 4 star experience more 3 star Room a little claustrophobic as it was at eye level with roof of courtyard covered bar   They did have a universal adaptor which I went down to collect from reception there was no offer to send it up Shower room clean

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: No complaints   We were traveling with our 2 kids 10 and 12 and booked the room with 2 full bathrooms It was perfect The bathrooms are large by hotel standards and the hotel was recently renovated so everything still was relatively new The price reflects the slightly off the beaten path location but you can reach great places in a 10 15 minute walk Would definitely recommend this hotel to family and friends

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: We had a great two nights at the Hotel L Echiquier Opera The room was a good size for Paris accomodation The room was so quiet and the bed was amazing Breakfast was excellent We will be back to this accomodation

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Didn t enjoy eating in the bar lounge better restaurants available locally Easy enough to get to from the airport but like most places in Paris involved a couple of tube changes   Room was spacious and comfortable

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: There was nothing we didn t like   The staff were all very helpful and happy to help The breakfast choice was very good The bed was very comfortable

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Parking was a bit of a challenge but I was informed that valet is available and previously it was not stated in the description of the facilities   Everything was great I love this hotel

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: The pillows were not very comfortable the hotel temperature was a bit hot  Good wi fi comfortable room very very nice staff hotel clean and tidy good mojito

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Rooms and breakfast are really expensive but worth every cent   Stayed there once before the renovation and must say that everything is better now especially the breakfast Good location close to two metro station close to cafes very nice and comfortable rooms and a very friendly and helpful staff All this made our stay very pleasant and will come back if we find a good deal someday

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: The location was a little out of the way by 2 or 3 minutes walking of where we wanted to be Only a problem because we should ve packed lighter   Best bed we slept on in Europe Very comfortable quiet and chic

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Rooms could be a little big bigger   I spend most of my life in h tels for my work and Hotel de l Echiquier has the best service I have seen for years Polite discreet very efficient and both respectful and not obsequious Location is also great to enjoy Parisian life in a quiet place

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Nothing   Service provided was excellent Every contact we had with the staff both at the reception and at the breakfast was pleasant The room we had was very small but comfortable I loved the pillows Everything we had for breakfast was delicious and fresh The location of the hotel is great you can get to the main spots of Paris all on foot

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Going home   Very attractive building superb extensive breakfast well appointed rooms professional friendly staff at a very reasonable price Great location for walking around many of the sights in Paris and good selection of bars and restaurants round about Convenient for Gare Du Nord we walked it in approx 10 mins We stayed six nights

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: The Staff especially the team that served us breakfast the 4 days were amazing and helpful On Valentine s day they helped me surprising my partner and more Great place the metro is near as well

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: no prunes for breakfast really thats all I can think of Everything else was really great   Fantastic pillow actually slept so well and room was quiet too and lovely staff too oh and so so clean none of the places I checked were dusty not even a tiny bit this is very rare

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: We thought breakfast was until 12 We couldnt eat much cuz they were starting to clean 10minbefore 11xD  Great welcoming and staffs were great We had a very good stay

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Breakfast was really good but still limited for kids   We stayed over the weekend and enjoyed our time in this hotel Though the location of the hotel is a little bit off from the key sights taxi didn t take us too long staff rooms were much better vs our expectations we would definitely come back

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Great staff great location 2 min from Metro super nice room very comfortable bed very clean The breakfast was really good the cocktails at the bar too although overpriced as usual at hotel bars We would probably stay at this hotel again next time we are in Paris

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: The variety at breakfast was superb with a lovely friendly service also Bedrooms lovely comfortable with it cleaned each day during our stay Also was very impressed with how clean the entire hotel was a very well organised hotel with excellent staff to match Wifi throughout the hotel was excellent as well Would recommend to anyone travelling to Paris

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Staff was friendly helpful nice Hotel location near la fayetter street Bonnee nouvelle metro station about 3 5 mins walking 4 mins by metro to the opera Alot of resturants around the hotel area All in all an excellent experience in my view

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: 1 the quality of the Clarins conditioner offered in the bathroom not moist enough for dry weather 2 minor soap residuals in the walk in shower   1 Friendly and polite front desk staffs 2 Clean and newly renovated guestrooms 3 The towel warmer rack in bath room my own towel was totally dried in the next morning thus I can put it back in my suitcase without hesitation and the possible bad smell 4 Walk in shower facilities is good for relaxing a whole day sightseeing in the city 5 Modern design guestroom 6 The antique feel elevator 7 Smart well dressed and reliable front desk staffs 8 Delicious breakfast buffet at affordable and reasonable price 9 Positive attitude of waiters le bar 38 willing to correct their unfavorable attitude after our voice out 10 sizeable and comfortable beds 12 offering paper coffee cup for travelers carring on the road impressive 11 nice and clean bathroom with adequate light 12 the service of baggage storage for few days 13 a smart way to offer a curtain to hide the guestroom door 14 the use of durable cloth for sofa is a smart way

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Would have been good to have breakfast included in the rate   The room was large by Parisian standards we took a junior suite as it was comparable in price to a triple room We had three single and very comfortable beds as requested Bathroom had separate shower and bath with all Clarins products The hotel was quiet and the staff at reception very welcoming and helpful Would definitely stay again as there are great little restaurants near by If you re looking to be in the thick of the Paris tourist district perhaps not the best location but if like us you have been to Paris several times and like to be in an area with a more local vibe then would highly recommend

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: The air con wasn t working fine for us in April but best check with the hotel it is fixed Free WiFi was okay but a bit of a pain registration process and slow there s a paid for premium service if you want it Check in staff was very friendly but the systems were a little slow Gym was compact 3 cardio machines and some free weights but nicely decorated For it s style could have done with a few kettlebells a barbell medicine balls etc   Great hotel friendly staff a really cool recent refurb in old Art Deco Parisien style Location is handy to the cool liveable side of Paris easy enough to get over the tourist spots and a 15 min walk from Gare du Nord It s a low cost Sofitel but the things they skimp on I was happy enough to do without Great 4 star option

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: None perfect stay  Beautifully renovated h tel in art deco style located in a quiet location Very friendly staff who go the extra mile to please us We booked the room with breakfast which was high quality dishes fresh fruits juices homemade pancakes breads bacon eggs baked beans Location was great 3 tube stations within walking distance 15 min by from gare de l est 20 min to st Germain 30 min to the foot of the Eiffel tower There are also plenty of restaurants cafes and grocery shops nearby I would not hesitate to go back as good hotels with decent room and bathroom in a well situated quarter of Paris offering value for money are difficult to find

Hotel_Name: H tel L Echiquier Op ra Paris MGallery by Sofitel
Full_Review: Nothing   Everything was perfect It s just a short distance from a number of metro lines so the location is ideal with easy access to Saint Germain des Pres Trocadero Montmartre and other areas The staff were very friendly particularly the breakfast restaurant staff The room was great and the bed was incredibly comfortable The breakfast was excellent and wide ranging with outstanding pastries They even had little touches like mini chocolate eggs under the tea cups for Easter All in all it was a brilliant stay

Full_Review: I have asked twice to change bed clothes and it was ignored We did not asked to change towels every day but one day we noticed it was one towel short so hope hotel did not think we took it There were no beverages in the fridge on our arrival thanks to an amazing receptionist who brought them later Just quick question are you re feeling the fridge with beverages Olga  On our arrival 4th March late at night there was absolutely amazing receptionist who provided so much support and information that it looked like we are the most and only welcomed guests in the whole hotel During other days other staff members were professional and polite but never gave that feeling of you are the most important guest in the hotel that that tall black hair receptionist with the glasses gave to us Very pity we do not know his name

The staff were rude and the concierge wouldn t give us any information about how to get around London The toilet was broken on arrival and it took three phone calls before anything was done and eventually somebody came up at 11 30pm so we had to wait up until they left before we could sleep and we had driven for 8 hours On the second day we got back at 3 30pm to get ready for going to the lion king that night however we got back to the room and housekeeping had not been in I called down to say we were in a rush and wanted showers with no clean towels it took them half an hour to get us towels meaning we didn t have time for a shower before we went out for the night The next day when we returned there was a handyman in the bathroom retiling the bathroom he left after using all the toilet roll for god knows what and the bathroom was a mess from the materials he was using to re tile There was only 1 lift working for the whole hotel for the duration of our stay which meant we had to wait for roughly 5 10 mins every time When house keeping did come I asked the staff member for towels and to not bother cleaning the room she said she didn t speak English and came into the room and started cleaning around us The room we were allocated too had boilers air vents outside that were very noisy all night so gave us a disturbed sleep When going for breakfast we put a do not disturb sign on the door as we had money ipads phones lying around and when we came back from breakfast someone was in our room Overall the stay was absolutely horrific and as a Hilton honours member I am so disappointed that I will probably never stay in a Hilton again   Sandra who worked in the bar

We were in an attic room not ideal if tall not reached by the lift which meant a lot of stairs so if you can t manage stairs let them know in advance to get a room near a lift The kitchenette would be useful for a longer stay I guess but it would have been better for us to have a coffee pod machine especially as the reception coffee machine was out of order   Less than 10 minutes walk from Paddington and yet peaceful location Walkable to Notting Hill and Kensington This is a grand old terraced building with comfortable though rather characterless rooms We got a free upgrade to an executive deluxe suite with a separate seating area which was great value for money The bed was very comfy the shower was hot but not that powerful no worse than most hotels though Good towels and bedding Staff were helpful and our room was made available for us early No breakfast but you could order a packed lunch type breakfast in your room We preferred to go out for breakfast but if you book direct on the hotel website there seem to be frequent free breakfast offers Overall much better than I would have expected for London at this price We will definitely stay here if we visit this part of London again

Nothing   Great location within walking distance from 3 underground stations Numerous cafes pubs restaurants at a short 3 7 mins walk The front desk staff were extremely helpful and accommodating Appreciated the all day Nespresso machine in the lobby Very comfortable 2 weeks Will definitely make this our base again

Queen size bed was a bit too big for the small room a single bed would have made better use of the space Water pressure top floor was low but that s a general London problem Redecoration in parts sloppy no functional problems but cracks here and there   Friendly staff good location 10 minute walk from Paddington room was quiet functional and well equipped House well kept with nice public areas Elevator available for most floors

The first night we had quite a cold draft coming from the window but the second night was better once the heating was turned up   The staff was friendly and the room was clean There was a little well equipped kitchen in our room however we didn t use it

Our room was located close to an exhaust or outdoor air vent which was quite noisy and meant we didn t get the best of sleeps on our first night We requested to be moved the following day albeit in the afternoon and the two rooms we were presented with had their deficiencies The first was a basement room wow lucky we didn t get that and the other had the same issue We kept our room The hairdryer was useless and there were no Ironing facilities which made for a difficult business trip The soaps and shampoos couldve been of better quality but was no means a deal breaker Pillows were also a little uncomfortable but that personal preference   Was in a good location close to Queensway and Bayswater tube stations The place was clean and the room we had was welcoming Shower had great water pressure and mattress was comfortable We could also adjust the temperature of the room which was handy as we dont like the heater to be too warm even though it s winter Nespresso coffee machine in the foyer went down a treat

The only problem in our perfect bedroom was that it had the loudest air conditioning in the world   The room we stayed at was beautiful The walls and the ceilings had those lovely flowery shapes with angels and leaves The room had an amazing window that was the size of the wall the bed was standing against It even had beautiful stained glasses above The bed was literally the best bed we ve ever slept in The room is spacious and cozy at the same time There s plenty of places to tidy your things in The bathroom was very clean and warm with big towels and a nice shower It had a very useful kitchenette with a mini fridge mini stove a sink a toaster and a microwave In short it was the best room we ve ever stayed at and a hotel that I d absolutely stay again at The location is great the staff are helpful and it even has a shelf with good books to borrow We just want to go back again

The bathroom was scruffy cracked tiles on the floor silicon which was stained in the shower and as you open the shower door the complete screen is lose at the base It is a shame because a few hours by one tradesman could over come most of these points The ductwork in the bedroom emits a clicking noise which gets on your nerves at night I thought it was the Air Con so turned it off but it wasn t it was coming from the fresh air ductwork into the room So really a typical London room at this price I was expecting better but it sits in that category of could do better   The room was good value at 80 00 for 1 night however it is January and London is quite I would not like to pay much more than this The bed was comfy Location for tubes is good

Lift in between floors was a slight problem with the many suitcases we had No exhaust in the shower room toilet also missed having a bath but that was not a big negative besides it was nice and clean   We enjoyed the visit Friendly and cooperative staff The room was clean and spacious for a family room so did not feel cramped Having a kitchenette was extremely handy as staying with a 2 month old baby for 4 days Kitchenette was well designed compact and could be hidden away when not in use Liked the dimmer lights over the bed as it helped with nighttime feeding The dressing table was a clever partition between the two double beds providing just enough privacy that we needed Happy location wise nice area situated in central London and not too far away from local amenities and restaurants Little add ons good pick of newspapers in the reception lounge enjoyed reading with a delicious cup of coffee from the Nespresso machine Will definitely consider returning

WiFi didn t work properly No cell phone network in the room which was in the basement   Staff was very very nice and helpful We reached the hotel pretty late in the evening and were too tired to head out too far Thus we asked the staff at the reception for suggestions and they were very helpful and showed us the directions on a map to an area that was a 10 minute walk from the hotel and had a lot of restaurants we could choose from The Wifi was not working and we didn t get network on our cell phones because our room was in the basement so they helped connect to their other networks including the staff network to ensure that we weren t inconvenienced However those didn t work too well either Because we were having issues with the Wifi and no cell phone network the reception staff even agreed to move us to a room on a higher floor without any extra charges however they got a last minute booking and it wasn t possible The day we were checking out we had tried to book a cab the previous day But it got cancelled because there weren t any cabs available and our booking was cancelled However because we had no phone reception we didn t know it was cancelled and were waiting for 30 minutes But as soon as we told the reception staff about this they arranged for a cab that got there within 15 20 minutes Room was nice and spacious and big enough for 3 people 1 double bed 1 single bed with 6 bags to stay in comfortably Bathroom was nothing fancy but nice and clean Kitchenette was very well equipped Overall the hotel was good and the staff was amazing But we checked out 2 days early because only a basement room was available where there was no network and the Wifi didn t work properly so we couldn t plan our day look stuff up or book tickets online It would be a pretty nice place to stay at as long as you get a room that is not in the basement It was a 10 12 minute walk from Paddington Station where the Heathrow Connect and Heathrow Express stop
